codeReview.summary: The websocket sync improves task refresh behavior, but the in-place update path trusts raw websocket payloads and can leave task objects missing normalized compatibility fields required elsewhere in the store.

issues.description: `extractTaskFromWebSocketMessage()` returns the websocket payload as-is instead of normalizing it. If the backend sends snake_case fields (for example `requester_id`, `checker_id`, `approver_id`, `current_node`, which the REST API normalizes via `normalizeReviewTask()`), `upsertReviewTask()` will store an object whose camelCase fields like `requesterId`/`currentNode` are undefined. That immediately breaks designer/reviewer filtering (`myInitiatedTasks`, `pendingReviewTasks`) and can also cause updated tasks to disappear or remain stale after a websocket event, which violates the auto-refresh requirement.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `designer-tracking-worker` skill requires TDD, read-only lint on changed files, and manual verification with agent-browser, but this worker implemented code before any new failing tests, ran repository-wide `npm run lint`, and recorded no interactive checks.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton for session `1f24729e-6ba4-42a4-b627-521bcb0a128d` shows `ApplyPatch` calls before new websocket tests were added, then `npm run lint`, and the handoff has `interactiveChecks: []` despite SKILL.md sections 3, 5, and 6 requiring TDD, read-only lint on changed files, and manual agent-browser verification.

summary: Fail: websocket notifications are wired up, but `task_updated`-style events persist raw websocket payloads without REST-style normalization, so snake_case backend payloads can break task ownership/status filtering and prevent the relevant lists from refreshing correctly.
